observations.text: 7 concrete artifacts in Preserving the Learning, each with specific file locations, detailed content descriptions, and named owners — far exceeds the 3-artifact minimum
ADR-019 (Artifact 1) documents the regulatory vs infrastructure durability distinction with MJ as owner and Bird as required reviewer
Bird's domain rule annotation (Artifact 2) explicitly codifies the synchronous commitment requirement with Bird as owner
Kobe's checklist update (Artifact 4) adds a specific domain rule compatibility check item with blocking semantics — exactly what the rubric requires

Clear distinction maintained between Preserving the Learning (codified artifacts) and Action Items (forward commitments) — no conflation
Bonus artifacts (glossary entry, quarterly review agenda, compliance runbook) add genuine value beyond minimum requirements

# Learning Review: FCA Audit Trail Non-Compliance (2026-01-19)

## 1. Situation Snapshot

On 2026-01-19, the FCA flagged that 6 weeks of order state transition records for regulated instruments were non-compliant with COBS 11.2A audit trail requirements.
